questions.question: 5 (a) Table 5.1 shows some properties of five halogens. Table 5.1 halogen melting point in °C boiling point in °C atomic volume in cm 3 / mol fluorine–220–188 chlorine–101–3522.7 bromine–7+5925.6 iodine+114+18425.8 astatine+33732.8 Use the information in Table 5.1 to predict: (i) the melting point of astatine ...............................................................................

parts.answer: copper(II) sulfate (1) blue (1) OR cobalt(II) chloride (1) red / pink (1) Question Answer Marks electrical conductor (1) thermal conductor (1) number of electrons in Cu 2+ = 27 (1) number of protons in Ca = 20 AND number of protons in Cu 2+ = 29 (1) nd box down ticked (They often act as catalysts.) 1 October/November 2024 Question Answer Marks if two marks not scored: one mark for one pair reversed OR reverse order

manganese(IV) oxide loses oxygen Question Answer Marks • heat to point of crystallisation / heat to form saturated solution • filter off crystals / pick out crystals • dry with filter paper + ZnO + (2)HCl on the line on the left AND ZnCl and H O on the line on the right than the energy (level) of the poly(ethene) / the energy of the products is lower than the energy of the reactants pH 12: blue (1) Question Answer Marks October/November 2024 Question Answer Marks 1.6 0.4 negative electrode: magnesium (1) irregular / no (particular) arrangement (1) motion: sliding (over each other) / moving over each other (1) separation: close together (1)
